content.question:
Choose the best alternative.

1. Did you notice the "___" sign outside the house next door?
content.final_notes_html:
📚 Ghi chú Từ vựng (Vocabulary Notes):

  • for sale = offered to be sold. (e.g. The car is for sale.)
    on sale = at a reduced price / available to be bought. (e.g. The magazine is on sale every Thursday.)
  • absorbed by = soaked up by. (e.g. Light is absorbed more by black than white.)
    absorbed in = fascinated by / spending a lot of time on sth. (e.g. Tom is absorbed in his studies.)
  • in possession of = owning. (e.g. The government is in possession of the file.)
    in the possession of = owned by. (e.g. The file is in the possession of the government.)
  • meddle with = interfere with objects. (e.g. Don't meddle with electricity.)
    meddle in = interfere with situations. (e.g. Don't meddle in other people's affairs.)
  • in sight = within someone's range of vision. (e.g. The helicopter came in sight.)
    on sight = as soon as sth is seen. (e.g. We were ordered to shoot the enemy on sight.)
  • advise sb of = inform sb about sth. (e.g. The bank advised me of my overdraft.)
    advise sb on = give sb advice about. (e.g. I asked the doctor to advise me on dieting.)
  • at hand = nearby, convenient. (e.g. I always keep some water at hand during the night.)
    in hand = being dealt with. (e.g. The matter in hand.)
  • support sb with = help sb by doing sth. (e.g. He helped to support the child with gifts of money.)
    support sb in = help sb in a particular situation. (e.g. Who will support me in this crisis?)
  • on sight = as soon as sth is seen.
    by sight = by the appearance. (e.g. We know him by sight but don't know his name.)
  • respond with = react in a certain way. (e.g. The audience responded with a round of applause.)
    respond to = react to sth. (e.g. Nobody responded to his cries for help.)

content.question:
Choose the best alternative.

2. There's no point trying to talk to Stuart; he's totally ___ that magazine.

Choose the best alternative.

3. He was arrested at the airport for being ___ a dangerous weapon.

Choose the best alternative.

4. The director of the firm received an anonymous letter, warning him not to ___ other people's business.

Choose the best alternative.

5. As soon as the leopard came ___, the hunter raised his rifle and shot.

Choose the best alternative.

6. This letter is to ___ the fact that your overdraft currently stands at £64.35.

Choose the best alternative.

7. Some people believe that the end of the world is ___.

Choose the best alternative.

8. The most upsetting part of her relationship with her parents was that they rarely ___ her decisions.

Choose the best alternative.

9. Even though they knew each other ___ they never exchanged words.

Choose the best alternative.

10. When the magician was asked the secret of his trick, he ___ a smile.
